title: Reading and Use of English – Part 8
description: You are going to read an article about four people who attended a wellness retreat. For questions 47–56, choose from the people A–D. The people may be chosen more than once.

texts.A:
### Liam, The Silent Retreat
'I’m a talkative person by nature, so ten days of silence was a daunting prospect,' Liam admits. [51]The most agonizing part was the lack of distraction; I couldn't hide from my own thoughts behind a screen or a conversation.[/51] [47]I was actually quite taken aback by how loud my own mind felt in the absence of external noise.[/47] [54]However, by day five, I experienced a sense of profound stillness that I hadn't felt in decades.[/54] [56]I wouldn't have even considered this if my therapist hadn't suggested it as a radical way to address my chronic anxiety.[/56] [49]It taught me that silence isn't a void, but a space for genuine self-discovery.[/49] I returned to work with a much calmer perspective.
texts.B:
### Sarah, The Hiking and Nutrition Retreat
Sarah spent a week in the mountains focusing on physical health. 'I was used to quick fixes and caffeine,' she says. [48]I was surprised to find that my persistent headaches vanished after just three days of clean eating and mountain air.[/48] [52]I hadn't anticipated the emotional release that came with pushing my physical limits on the steeper trails.[/52] [50]The workshop on meal planning was surprisingly practical; I’ve successfully integrated those habits into my busy family life back home.[/50] [53]The retreat forced me to realize that I’d been neglecting my body's basic needs for years in favor of my career.[/53] [47]The transition to an early sleep schedule was a shock to my system, yet I adapted within forty-eight hours.[/47] It was a total reset.
texts.C:
### Hiroko, The Meditation and Yoga Retreat
Hiroko attended a coastal retreat after a period of burnout. 'I felt like a broken engine,' she recalls. [49]The experience helped me see that my worth isn't tied to my productivity, which was a liberating realization.[/49] [51]I struggled initially with the physical discomfort of sitting still for hours, which I found deeply frustrating.[/51] [55]It took the entire week for me to stop reaching for my phone every few minutes, a habit that was harder to break than I’d like to admit.[/55] [50]I found that the focus on mindfulness helped me regain the creativity I thought I’d lost forever.[/50] [54]The sound of the waves during our dawn sessions provided a healing rhythm that I still visualize today.[/54] It was truly life-changing.
texts.D:
### James, The Digital Detox Retreat
James went to a remote cabin with no technology. 'I felt naked without my watch and phone,' he laughs. [56]If I hadn't lost a bet with my brother, I never would have agreed to disconnect so completely.[/56] [53]The experience made me aware of how much of my anxiety was fueled by the constant stream of global news.[/53] [52]I hadn't realized how much I’d missed the simple pleasure of reading a physical book without interruptions.[/52] [48]I noticed a significant improvement in my eye strain and posture after just a few days away from screens.[/48] [55]The first forty-eight hours were filled with a strange sense of boredom that I had to learn to sit with.[/55] I’ve now implemented 'tech-free Sundays' at home.

questions.question: mentions being surprised by their internal or physical reaction to a new environment?
questions.explanation: Liam (A) was taken aback by how loud his mind felt, and Sarah (B) was shocked by the sleep schedule transition.

questions.question: describes a physical benefit that occurred more rapidly than expected?
questions.explanation: Sarah (B) mentions headaches vanishing in three days, and James (D) mentions eye strain improving in a few days.

questions.question: expresses a fundamental change in their self-perception or philosophy?
questions.explanation: Liam (A) learned about silence as a space for discovery, and Hiroko (C) realized her worth isn't tied to productivity.

questions.question: notes that a skill or habit from the retreat has been successfully maintained?
questions.explanation: Sarah (B) integrated meal planning into family life, and Hiroko (C) regained her creativity.

questions.question: mentions a specific type of discomfort they had to face during the experience?
questions.explanation: Liam (A) faced the lack of distraction/his own thoughts, and Hiroko (C) faced physical discomfort from sitting.

questions.question: acknowledges an unexpected emotional or psychological discovery?
questions.explanation: Sarah (B) found emotional release through hiking, and James (D) rediscovered the pleasure of reading.

questions.question: reflects on a negative habit or lifestyle choice they hadn't fully recognized before?
questions.explanation: Sarah (B) realized she'd been neglecting her body, and James (D) saw how news fueled his anxiety.

questions.question: describes a moment of peace that had a lasting impact on their memory?
questions.explanation: Liam (A) felt a sense of stillness on day five, and Hiroko (C) still visualizes the sound of the waves.

questions.question: mentions the difficulty of breaking a deep-seated modern habit?
questions.explanation: Hiroko (C) struggled to stop reaching for her phone, and James (D) mentions the struggle with boredom.

questions.question: reveals that their participation was prompted by an external influence?
questions.explanation: Liam (A) was prompted by his therapist, and James (D) by a bet with his brother.
